intent: Create an enterprise Agent Trust Fabric profile for SecurityRecipes that turns identity, behavior, context, least-privilege scope, telemetry, containment, source freshness, and buyer proof into one deterministic trust verdict before an agent retrieves secure context, invokes MCP tools, delegates, writes memory, or performs high-impact actions.

positioning.buyer: AI platform, product security, CISO, GRC, model-risk, SOC, procurement-security, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can answer the board-level question 'which agents can we trust right now?' with generated evidence, source hashes, runtime decisions, and MCP tools instead of narrative policy.
positioning.why_now: 2026 agent deployments are moving from pilots to autonomous action. Current guidance converges on the same enterprise requirement: every agent must have verifiable identity, least-privilege scope, governed context input and output, real-time behavior telemetry, and fast containment.

source_references.name: ATF: Zero Trust for AI Agents
source_references.why_it_matters: CSA frames agent security around identity, behavior, data governance, least privilege, and containment, which matches the trust-fabric dimensions SecurityRecipes can enforce through MCP-readable evidence.

source_references.name: AI Controls Matrix
source_references.why_it_matters: CSA AICM gives enterprise buyers a broader AI governance and shared-responsibility control baseline that the trust fabric can map into operational agent checks.

source_references.name: AI Agent Standards Initiative
source_references.why_it_matters: NIST is prioritizing trusted, interoperable, secure agent standards; SecurityRecipes needs a cross-vendor trust decision shape rather than a vendor-specific prompt policy.

source_references.name: CAISI Request for Information on Securing AI Agent Systems
source_references.why_it_matters: CAISI highlights indirect prompt injection, poisoning, misaligned actions, and constrained deployment access as distinct agent security risks.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: The current MCP authorization spec requires protected-resource metadata, scope challenges, resource indicators, token audience validation, and token-passthrough denial for protected MCP servers.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol 2025-11-25 Key Changes
source_references.why_it_matters: The latest MCP revision adds incremental scope consent, URL-mode elicitation, OAuth client ID metadata documents, and experimental tasks that affect trust decisions for long-running agent work.

source_references.name: OWASP MCP Top 10
source_references.why_it_matters: OWASP MCP risks such as token exposure, scope creep, tool poisoning, command execution, insufficient auth, shadow MCP servers, and over-sharing define trust-fabric failure modes.

source_references.name: OpenAI Agents SDK Guardrails
source_references.why_it_matters: OpenAI's guardrail guidance distinguishes first-input, final-output, and per-function-tool guardrails, making runtime placement and hosted-tool gaps explicit trust-fabric concerns.

source_references.name: OpenAI Agents SDK Tracing
source_references.why_it_matters: Agent trust cannot be reconstructed without traces that capture LLM generations, tool calls, handoffs, guardrails, and custom events.

source_references.name: OpenTelemetry GenAI Agent and Framework Spans
source_references.why_it_matters: OpenTelemetry GenAI conventions provide cross-vendor span names and attributes for agent invocation and tool execution, giving the trust fabric a portable telemetry substrate.

trust_dimensions.title: Verifiable Agent Identity
trust_dimensions.question: Who is the agent and which delegated identity is it using?

trust_dimensions.title: Trusted Context Ingress
trust_dimensions.question: What is the agent consuming and is that context safe to trust?

trust_dimensions.title: Least-Privilege Scope and Action Boundary
trust_dimensions.question: Where can the agent go and which action class is being attempted?

trust_dimensions.title: Runtime Behavior Monitoring
trust_dimensions.question: What is the agent doing in real time and does it match its declared intent?

trust_dimensions.title: Context Output and Egress Governance
trust_dimensions.question: What is the agent serving or sending outside the trust boundary?

trust_dimensions.title: Containment and Recovery
trust_dimensions.question: What happens if the agent goes off-policy?

trust_tiers.title: Intern
trust_tiers.promotion_gate: Identity registered, telemetry present, no high-risk tool access, and context source freshness is current.

trust_tiers.title: Apprentice
trust_tiers.promotion_gate: Context trust, poisoning scan, least-privilege scopes, and receipt evidence are complete for the workflow.

trust_tiers.title: Operator
trust_tiers.promotion_gate: Action runtime, authorization, egress, telemetry, approval, and receipt evidence are bound to the run.

trust_tiers.title: Principal
trust_tiers.promotion_gate: Separate risk acceptance, containment proof, red-team replay evidence, and customer-specific hosted MCP controls are present.

step_up_required_when: requested_trust_tier is operator or principal and human approval is absent
authorization_decision, egress_decision, action_runtime_decision, or telemetry_decision is hold
deny_when: requested trust tier exceeds generated workflow tier and no approval exists
required evidence fields are missing after normalization
trust score falls below deny threshold
kill_when: delegated identity was used after revocation
secret egress or cross-tenant context access is observed
prompt injection is combined with external side effect

commercialization_path.open_layer: Publish the Agent Trust Fabric pack as open evidence so teams can understand the trust decision model and fork it into their own control plane.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Sell hosted trust-fabric APIs that combine customer-private identity, telemetry, authorization, egress, approval, and receipt data into signed MCP-readable trust verdicts.
commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab, AI platform, or security vendor gets a standards-backed agent trust surface that can sit between agents, MCP tools, context stores, telemetry, and trust centers.

executive_readout.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes can reduce agent adoption friction by turning many scattered agent safety controls into one inspectable trust verdict per run.
executive_readout.recommended_first_use: Use the open pack for AI platform architecture review, then attach the evaluator to MCP gateway decisions and customer pilot telemetry.
executive_readout.why_it_is_differentiated: Most agent guidance stops at controls. The trust fabric composes generated evidence into an allow, hold, deny, or kill decision that agents and auditors can consume.
